extracted.brandBackground: Mathnasium operates learning centers that provide math instruction using the Mathnasium system of learning.

leadership.background: Served as Chief Operating Officer of MCL from January 2023 to July 2024.

item19.notes: Includes existing franchised Mathnasium Centers in the United States that were open and operated by the same franchisee for 12 months or longer as of December 31, 2025.

lineItems.notes: Standard initial franchise fee for the first center.
Covers travel and lodging costs for training.
Covers technology license fees before opening.
Estimated rent for a 1,200 to 1,600 square foot space.
Includes exterior signs, interior wall art, desks, and chairs.
Annual premium for required insurance coverage.
Legal and accounting services to start the business.
Setup deposits and initial utility costs.
Required local marketing spend before opening.
Working capital for the initial operational period.

hiddenCosts.name: Late fee for late payment
hiddenCosts.description: $250, plus an additional $100 every 2 weeks until payment is made.

hiddenCosts.name: Non-Compliance Fine
hiddenCosts.description: $300 per month for each non-complying incident.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation, arbitration, or litigation in Los Angeles County, California.

operationalRisks.title: Sales Performance Required
operationalRisks.description: Must maintain minimum enrollment of 75 students and not fall below the 8th percentile of gross revenue.

extracted.conceptRationale: Mathnasium is a learning center providing math instruction to children.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires a Center Director and instructors to teach students.

riskReasons: A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $146,581 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $17,930/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: Mathnasium is a learning center providing math instruction to children.

benchmark.label: Education / childcare
benchmark.dominantRisk: Regulated staff ratios and enrollment/utilization. Labor dominates and ratios cap how lean you can run.
considerations: Labor is the dominant line at ~40–55% and is floored by regulated student/staff ratios.
Validate enrollment ramp and capacity-utilization curves with Item 20 operators.
Confirm licensing, facility, and compliance costs specific to your state — not in the FDD.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 3.1–4.3 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Education / childcare” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Taken from the FDD (~$4,092/mo); already netted in margin after fees & rent.
Estimated at the 5–12% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 40–55% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: What we found (2025-12-31, audited by PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP): Net income of $40.88M on $53.42M of revenue — roughly 0.2x spent per $1 earned. Positive net worth of $338.40M. Cash of $576K against $4.60M of obligations due within a year (a current ratio of 2.58). Note: about 74% of those liabilities is deferred revenue — prepaid franchise fees the company owes as future services, not borrowed money. Netting it out, liabilities are still about 0.0x assets.

Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so Mathnasium Holdings, LLC is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
